FS.title: Frontier Scout
FS.contribution: You discover ignored questions, weak signals, and new possibilities before others see them.
FS.risk: You may chase novelty permanently and abandon construction before it matures.
FS.experiment: Formulate three falsifiable questions and talk to seven people from different perspectives.

SC.title: Systems Cartographer
SC.contribution: You make complexity visible — relationships, incentives, and second-order effects that others miss.
SC.risk: You may turn the map into a substitute for decision, refining the model after the problem is clear enough for experiment.
SC.experiment: Publish a map of actors, incentives, feedback loops, and three essential unknowns.

EB.title: Experimental Builder
EB.contribution: You turn hypotheses into prototypes and evidence, making abstract ideas testable.
EB.risk: You may build too fast — solutions for the wrong problem, or prototypes that outpace understanding.
EB.experiment: Build a prototype that can invalidate your riskiest assumption within 30 days.

MO.title: Mission Operator
MO.contribution: You turn purpose into priorities, rhythm, and accountability — making things actually happen.
MO.risk: You may confuse metrics and process with the mission itself, optimizing delivery while losing direction.
MO.experiment: Establish a four-week cycle with owner, outcome, metric, and stopping condition.

CW.title: Community Weaver
CW.contribution: You create trust, alliances, and cooperation across differences that would otherwise remain fragmented.
CW.risk: You may avoid necessary conflict and dilute accountability in the name of harmony.
CW.experiment: Convene five actors and produce a document of agreements, disagreements, and possible contributions.

MT.title: Meaning Translator
MT.contribution: You make ideas intelligible, memorable, and culturally relevant — bridging knowledge and action.
MT.risk: The story may arrive before the truth, or the narrative may outpace the evidence.
MT.experiment: Publish an explanation and test what three different audiences actually understood.

IA.title: Institutional Architect
IA.contribution: You design governance, incentives, and continuity structures that outlast individual effort.
IA.risk: You may produce bureaucracy before the problem is understood, or structure before trust.
IA.experiment: Draft a one-page document defining decision rights, accountability, ownership, and exit.

BS.title: Boundary Steward
BS.contribution: You protect limits, integrity, and stopping conditions that ambition prefers to ignore.
BS.risk: You may turn prudence into a permanent veto right, blocking progress that requires calculated risk.
BS.experiment: Define three non-negotiable boundaries and verify one ignored side effect.

independentStudio.title: Independent Venture Studio
independentStudio.character: Small team, autonomy, early revenue, fast experiments.

frontierResearchLab.title: Frontier Research Lab
frontierResearchLab.character: High uncertainty, long horizon, deep evidence, patient capital.

missionStartup.title: Mission Startup
missionStartup.character: Speed, distribution, market, external capital, scaling ambition.

openCommonsNetwork.title: Open Commons Network
openCommonsNetwork.character: Open knowledge, protocols, distributed governance, reuse.

communityVenture.title: Community Venture
communityVenture.character: Local trust, long-term relationships, resilience, deliberate limits.

cooperativeEnterprise.title: Cooperative Enterprise
cooperativeEnterprise.character: Shared ownership, real market, distributed decision-making.

publicInterestInstitution.title: Public-Interest Institution
publicInterestInstitution.character: Public legitimacy, continuity, standards, long horizon.

ecosystemOrchestrator.title: Ecosystem Orchestrator
ecosystemOrchestrator.character: Cross-organization alliances, shared infrastructure, systemic change.

complementarityPairs.description: One opens the questions; the other turns direction into rhythm. Likely tension: continuous exploration versus premature commitment. Agreement: separately define exploration time and decision point.
One finds the frontier; the other builds on it. Likely tension: too many open questions versus too-fast prototyping. Agreement: agree on which hypothesis to test first.
One maps the system; the other tests it. Likely tension: mapping forever versus building before understanding. Agreement: define the minimum map needed before the first prototype.
One sees the structure; the other makes it communicable. Likely tension: complexity versus simplification. Agreement: agree on what must never be simplified.
One builds fast; the other protects limits. Likely tension: speed versus safety. Agreement: define stopping conditions before building starts.
One drives execution; the other guards what must not be sacrificed. Likely tension: delivery pressure versus integrity. Agreement: define non-negotiable limits before the sprint begins.
One builds trust; the other builds rhythm. Likely tension: relationship time versus delivery time. Agreement: allocate explicit time for each.
One builds participation; the other builds structure. Likely tension: emergent process versus designed governance. Agreement: co-design the decision procedure.
One tells the story; the other maps the system. Likely tension: narrative appeal versus structural accuracy. Agreement: verify the story against the map before publishing.
One frames the meaning; the other tests the reality. Likely tension: story before evidence versus evidence without story. Agreement: iterate narrative and prototype in parallel.
One designs the rules; the other builds the trust to follow them. Likely tension: structure before trust versus trust before structure. Agreement: start with minimal rules and expand as trust grows.
One builds governance; the other protects limits. Likely tension: expanding structure versus constraining it. Agreement: embed limits into governance from the start.
One guards the limits; the other pushes past them. Likely tension: protection versus exploration. Agreement: define the boundary, then explore freely within it.
One sets the constraints; the other tests within them. Likely tension: constraint versus speed. Agreement: constraints are set before building, not during.

experiments.operating: Identify the mechanism that distorts the mission. {experiment}
experiments.scaling: Fix limits and governance before growth. {experiment}
experiments.supporting: Find the missing role and fund a small experiment. {experiment}

shareTexts.invite: What role do you play when an important idea has to become real?
shareTexts.minimal: I took the Outfinity Quiz. My constellation is {primaryRole} × {secondaryRole}. What role do you play?
shareTexts.full:
I took the Outfinity Quiz.
My constellation is {primaryRole} × {secondaryRole}.
Best ecology: {ecology}
What role do you play when an important idea has to become real?

recommendation.label: Outfinity initiative
recommendation.title: Explorer Circle
recommendation.text: Explorer Circle is a community for people who research difficult problems, build limited experiments, and look for collaborators with complementary roles. You do not need to share your result to participate.
recommendation.note: Opening Explorer Circle will take you to Substack. Your result is not transmitted. You can choose whether to mention it in the community.
